steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures at the boundary
steps.content: The left side completes an independent clause: “...the curve can be used to determine whether tax cuts will ultimately increase a country's tax revenue, according to some [economists]” (subject: curve; verb: can be used). The right side is an independent clause: “2017 data suggested that South Korea was below the threshold for a tax cut” (subject: data; verb: suggested).

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or a comma + coordinating conjunction. Choice D uses a period (“economists. The”) to correctly separate these two independent clauses into two complete sentences. The other choices violate this rule: A uses a comma alone (comma splice), B has no punctuation (run-on), and C uses a semicolon before a dependent clause introduced by “as” (semicolons cannot precede dependent clauses). That matches choice D — D is correct.
